prompt: A detailed 19th-century naturalist scientific illustration, hand-drawn lithograph style with delicate cross-hatching and a muted, natural color palette of sepia, indigo, and ochre on aged cream paper. The image is a geological cross-section of a rugged, complex mountain landscape. A series of six highly-detailed iridescent armored beetles are pinned at different elevations along a precise, winding downhill trajectory. Each subsequent beetle is pinned closer to the last. The final beetle rests at the edge of a deep, crystal-clear circular lake at the lowest point of the valley. The entire illustration is composed like a scientific plate, physically plausible, in the style of 19th-century naturalists. No charts, no modern notation, no digital artifacts. 8k resolution.
tradition_approach: Hand-drawn lithograph with cross-hatching and muted natural pigments
